title: Mira and the Fluttering Dreams
cover_prompt: A small, round dream-catcher named Mira with delicate silver wings, perched on a child’s windowsill. Soft moonlight spills through the window, illuminating her as she watches over a sleeping child. Tiny glowing dreams flutter around her like fireflies, their wings shimmering in pastel blues and golds. The scene is warm, cozy, and bathed in a dreamy watercolor glow.
lyrics:
The dreams are out tonight
Softly flutter, soft and light
Wings like whispers, silver bright
Looking for a sleepy sight

One just tapped upon your pane
Danced a jig then flew again
Almost stayed but heard the rain
Turned and waved and off it ran

[chorus]
Close your eyes and they will come
Dreams will tiptoe, soft and numb
Wrap you up so safe and warm
Better close your eyes, my one

See that dream with golden threads
Braided clouds inside its head
It will weave you stories spread
If you rest your sleepy head

[chorus]
Close your eyes and they will come
Dreams will tiptoe, soft and numb
Wrap you up so safe and warm
Better close your eyes, my one

Better close your eyes
style_prompt: Playful mysterious lullaby, warm female vocal, soft woodwind and low flute, 64 BPM, mischievous, conspiratorial, whimsical, fairy tale energy, like sharing a secret, mysterious but gentle, storytelling melody, soft woodwind accompaniment

character.name: Mira
character.identity: A gentle dream-catcher who guards the night and invites dreams to visit sleeping children.
character.special: She knows every dream by name and guides them to the right child with her soft, whispering wings.

about: A playful, conspiratorial lullaby where dreams flutter just out of reach, encouraging little ones to close their eyes to invite them in. The gentle rhythm of curiosity and warmth helps ease the fear of missing out (FOMO) by promising that dreams will come—if only they drift off first. Perfect for children who need a little nudge to let go of the day.
